attacking_style: Early breakaway specialist on flat or transitional stages, typically launching before categorized climbs when peloton motivation is low. Lacks power to sustain efforts over extended climbing.
team_tactics: Opportunistic early breaks on stages where GC teams prefer energy conservation. Minimal domestique support visible, suggesting independent breakaway attempts rather than coordinated team plays.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling terrain before mountain stages where peloton reluctance creates breakaway opportunities. Struggles significantly on categorized climbs, particularly medium-mountain passes like Colle di Nava.
vulnerability: Gets dropped when breakaways encounter sustained climbing. Insufficient power reserves to survive in breaks once gradient increases, exposing limited climbing capacity on even moderate ascents.
key_patterns: Targets early breaks on flat stages preceding mountain days when peloton conserves energy (2 instances)
Gets dropped from breakaways on categorized climbs, unable to sustain climbing efforts (2 instances)
Operates without significant team support in breakaway attempts, suggesting opportunistic tactics (2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: limited data but consistent pattern of gaining breaks then struggling on climbs suggests stable but limited climbing capacity rather than declining form
